@@ -126,6 +126,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-egf5","title":"TASK: Update /wizard/ssh-connect for password-first connection","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t66c (Wizard Password-First Flow Updates) ## Summary Update the SSH Connect wizard page to use password-based connection command instead of key-based. Users will SSH as root with password for their first connection. ## Current State Primary command shown: \`ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@IP\` This fails if the user didn't successfully add their SSH key to the provider. ## Target State Primary command: \`ssh root@IP\` With clear expectation that password will be requested. ## Implementation Details ### File: apps/web/app/wizard/ssh-connect/page.tsx ### Key Changes 1. **Update primary command** \`\`\`typescript const sshCommand = \`ssh root@\${vpsIP}\`; // Remove the -i flag and key path // Remove ubuntu user, use root \`\`\` 2. **Add password expectation** \`\`\`tsx When you run this command, it will ask for your password. This is the root password from your VPS provider. \`\`\` 3. **Update success indicator** Change expected prompt from \`ubuntu@vps:~$\` to \`root@vps:~#\` 4. **Update SimplerGuide** - Add step: "Enter your password when prompted" - Explain: "Nothing will appear as you type - that's normal" - Clarify: "This is the last time you'll need the password" 5. **Update troubleshooting** - "Wrong password" - check VPS provider email/panel - "Connection refused" - VPS still booting, wait 2 min - Remove SSH key specific errors from primary list ### New Password Guidance \`\`\`tsx When prompted, type your root password and press Enter.

Important: As you type, nothing will appear on screen. This is a security feature, not a bug! Just type your password and press Enter. \`\`\` ### Fallback Section Keep but reframe: > **Ubuntu user instead of root?** > Some providers use 'ubuntu' as the default user. If root doesn't work, try: > \`ssh ubuntu@IP\` > You may still need a password, or the provider might have set up a key. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Primary command is password-based (no -i flag) - [ ] Uses root user by default - [ ] Password expectation clearly stated - [ ] "Nothing appears when typing" explained - [ ] Expected prompt shows root@ - [ ] Troubleshooting updated for password flow - [ ] SimplerGuide steps updated - [ ] Mobile layout verified ## Testing 1. Follow instructions with fresh password-only VPS 2. Verify command works 3. Verify password prompt appears as described 4. Check troubleshooting covers common password issues ## Dependencies - TASK: Update /wizard/create-vps (should come first so flow is coherent) ## Files Modified - apps/web/app/wizard/ssh-connect/page.tsx","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:20:22.454663-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:35:05.365902-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:35:05.365902-05:00","close_reason":"Completed (page already matches password-first flow)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-egf5","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5yty","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:20:50.121846-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-eli","title":"Research CASS session data format and API","description":"# Task: Research CASS session data format and API ## Context Part of EPIC: Agent Session Sharing and Replay (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0sb) ## Why This Comes First Before we can export sessions, we need to understand how CASS stores them: - What format is the session data? - What commands does CASS expose? - What fields are available for export? - Are there multiple session types (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini)? ## Research Questions 1. Run 'cass --help' and document available commands 2. Run 'cass list' and examine output format 3. Run 'cass get --json' and examine structure 4. Check if CASS stores sessions per-agent or unified 5. Identify what data is NOT stored (we can't export what doesn't exist) ## Deliverables - Document CASS CLI commands and their output - Example session data structure - List of exportable fields - Any limitations or gaps discovered ## Acceptance Criteria - CASS API documented - Session format understood - Export schema can be designed based on actual data - Gaps identified for follow-up ## Notes This is research, not implementation. Output is documentation that informs subsequent tasks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:13.705596-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:52:01.46799-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:52:01.46799-05:00","close_reason":"Research complete. Documented CASS session format, CLI commands, search/export APIs, session storage locations, and identified 7 limitations. Created docs/cass-session-format-research.md."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-epc","title":"[codex.1] Fix doctor.sh codex auth detection","description":"## Goal Update scripts/lib/doctor.sh to correctly detect codex-cli authentication via OAuth tokens instead of checking for OPENAI_API_KEY. ## Current (Incorrect) Behavior The check_codex_auth() function currently: 1. Checks for OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable 2. Looks for OPENAI_API_KEY in ~/.zshrc.local 3. Looks for OPENAI_API_KEY in /data/projects/.envrc 4. Reports "pass" if any of these exist ## Correct Behavior Should instead: 1. Check for ~/.codex/auth.json (or similar OAuth token file) 2. Optionally verify the token isn't expired 3. Report "pass" if OAuth credentials exist 4. Suggest running "codex auth" if not authenticated ## Technical Notes - Need to verify the actual path where codex-cli stores OAuth tokens - The token file location may vary by OS/version - Common locations: ~/.codex/, ~/.config/codex/, ~/.local/share/codex/ ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Update check_codex_auth() function ## Acceptance Criteria 1. No mention of OPENAI_API_KEY in codex auth check 2. Correctly detects OAuth authentication 3. Provides helpful guidance for unauthenticated users 4. Regenerate scripts/generated/doctor_checks.sh after changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:35.325519-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:37:13.931458-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:37:13.931458-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed check_codex_auth to detect OAuth tokens in ~/.codex/auth.json instead of checking OPENAI_API_KEY env var. Also cleaned up redundant shell redirects.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-epc","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:35.68967-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-epc","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-wy1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:37.126029-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-f5m0","title":"Update manifest generator to include Claude fallback pattern and security fixes","description":"The install_agents.sh file is generated from acfs.manifest.yaml but has been manually customized with: (1) Fallback pattern that tries verified install first, then falls back to direct curl install, (2) Robust associative array check using grep for 'declare -A', (3) Safe variable initialization before array access. These customizations will be LOST if someone runs 'bun run generate'. The generator in packages/manifest/src/generate.ts should be updated to include this pattern so regeneration preserves these improvements.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:40:18.360744-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T02:40:18.360744-05:00"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fac","title":"EPIC: Project Foundation & Infrastructure","description":"# Epic: Project Foundation & Infrastructure ## Overview Set up the foundational infrastructure for the ACFS project, including repo structure, tooling, and development environment. ## Background & Reasoning Before any feature development can begin, we need a solid foundation: - Proper monorepo structure for website + installer + onboard - Consistent tooling (bun, TypeScript, linting) - CI/CD pipeline for quality gates - Documentation standards ## Success Criteria - [ ] All directory structure matches AGENTS.md spec - [ ] bun workspace configured properly - [ ] CI runs on every PR - [ ] shellcheck passes on all bash scripts ## Dependencies None - this is the foundation ## Considerations - Keep structure minimal initially, add complexity only when needed - Prefer convention over configuration - Make it easy for agents to understand and navigate ## Relationship to Project Goals This enables all subsequent development by providing a consistent, well-documented codebase structure that both humans and AI agents can work with effectively.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:19:59.763401-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:24:12.701537-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:24:12.701537-05:00","close_reason":"Foundation complete: directory structure, install.sh, library scripts, configs all created","labels":["epic","infrastructure"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","title":"EPIC: Module Manifest System","description":"# Epic: Module Manifest System ## Overview Create the module manifest system (acfs.manifest.yaml) that serves as single source of truth for all installed tools. ## Background & Reasoning Without a manifest, we'd have: - Scattered install logic - No way to generate doctor checks automatically - Website and installer out of sync - Hard to add/update tools The manifest provides: - Canonical list of all tools - Install commands - Verify commands - Dependencies between modules - Metadata for website display ## Manifest Structure ```yaml version: 1 name: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup id: acfs defaults: user: ubuntu workspace_root: /data/projects mode: vibe modules: - id: stack.ntm description: Named tmux manager (agent cockpit) category: stack priority: 1 install: - curl -fsSL https://... | bash verify: - ntm --help aliases: - ntm docs_url: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm ``` ## Generated Outputs 1. **Website**: Tools checklist on status page 2. **Installer**: Module installation scripts 3. **Doctor**: Check definitions with IDs 4. **Documentation**: Auto-generated tool reference ## Success Criteria - [ ] Complete manifest covering all 40+ tools - [ ] Parser library in packages/manifest - [ ] Generator for website content - [ ] Generator for installer modules - [ ] Generator for doctor checks - [ ] CI validates manifest syntax ## Considerations - Keep YAML human-readable - Use consistent IDs across the project - Document the schema thoroughly - Version the manifest format","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:39.336054-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:55:29.319623-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:55:29.319623-05:00","close_reason":"EPIC COMPLETE: All 3 children closed. Created manifest system with acfs.manifest.yaml, TypeScript parser library, and installer generator producing 13 scripts.","labels":["epic","manifest"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.1","title":"Create acfs.manifest.yaml with all modules","description":"# Task: Create Complete Module Manifest ## Description Create the canonical manifest file with all 40+ tool definitions. ## File: acfs.manifest.yaml ## Structure ```yaml version: 1 name: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup id: acfs defaults: user: ubuntu workspace_root: /data/projects mode: vibe categories: - id: base name: System Fundamentals priority: 1 - id: shell name: Shell Environment priority: 2 # ... etc modules: # Base system - id: base.curl description: HTTP client category: base install: [sudo apt-get install -y curl] verify: [curl --version] # ... 40+ modules ``` ## Module Categories 1. **base** - System packages (curl, git, jq, etc.) 2. **users** - User/permissions setup 3. **shell** - zsh, oh-my-zsh, plugins, theme 4. **cli** - Modern CLI tools (lsd, bat, fzf, etc.) 5. **lang** - Language runtimes (bun, uv, rust, go) 6. **tools** - Dev tools (atuin, tmux, ast-grep) 7. **db** - Databases (postgres18) 8. **cloud** - Cloud CLIs (vault, wrangler, supabase, vercel) 9. **agents** - Coding agents (claude, codex, gemini) 10. **stack** - Dicklesworthstone tools (8 tools) ## Each Module Contains - id: Stable identifier - description: Short description - category: Category reference - install: List of install commands - verify: List of verify commands - aliases: Optional shell aliases - docs_url: Documentation link - dependencies: Other module IDs ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 40+ modules defined - [ ] Valid YAML syntax - [ ] All verify commands documented - [ ] Dependencies specified - [ ] Categories organized logically","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:11.851933-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:00.620729-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:11:00.620729-05:00","close_reason":"acfs.manifest.yaml exists with 287 lines covering all tools","labels":["manifest","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:11.853698-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
